name: Escapade between Tarbes and Saint-Gaudens
region_intro: Touring cycling around La Barthe-De-Neste is characterized by its location at the foot of the Lannemezan Plateau and at the entrance to the Neste and Aure valleys. The region features a diverse landscape, ranging from rolling countryside and forests to the foothills of the Pyrenees. This area offers varied terrain, including peaceful roads through valleys and challenging ascents towards mountain passes. Best touring cycling routes around La Barthe-De-Neste The most popular touring cycling route is Escapade between Tarbes and Saint-Gaudens, a 43.3 miles (69.7 km) difficult trail that takes 4 hours 26 minutes to complete. This route connects two significant towns, offering a journey through the region's varied landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Palomières Pass – Col de Coupe loop from Lannemezan, a difficult 50.3 miles (81.0 km) path. This route includes notable passes, providing views of the Pyrenees and featuring numerous switchbacks. Local touring cyclists also love the Couret d'Asque – View of Laborde loop from Avezac, a 20.7 miles (33.4 km) trail leading through the Pyrenean foothills and rolling countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 25 minutes. Touring cycling around La Barthe-De-Neste is defined by Pyrenean peaks, river valleys, and rolling countryside, with routes offering options for easy rides through valleys and challenging climbs towards mountain passes. The routes in La Barthe-De-Neste are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore La Barthe-De-Neste's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around La Barthe-De-Neste?
A: There are over 90 touring cycling routes in the La Barthe-De-Neste region, offering a diverse range of experiences from leisurely rides through valleys to challenging ascents in the Pyrenean foothills.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers 6 easy touring cycling routes. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, many of the easier paths through rolling countryside and valleys are suitable for a relaxed ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6981207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montréjeau Lake Beach – Shortcut cycle route V81 loop from Lannemezan&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that follows part of the Vélosud shared route, known for peaceful roads.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 55 difficult touring cycling routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26227212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palomières Pass – Col de Coupe loop from Lannemezan&lt;/a&gt;, which is 50.3 miles (81 km) long and features significant elevation gain with numerous switchbacks and views of the Pyrenees. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868593478/escapade-between-tarbes-and-saint-gaudens" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escapade between Tarbes and Saint-Gaudens&lt;/a&gt;, a 43.3-mile (69.7 km) journey through varied landscapes.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: The touring cycling routes around La Barthe-De-Neste offer diverse scenery, from the rolling countryside and forests of oak, beech, and Oregon firs to the dramatic foothills of the Pyrenees. You'll encounter stunning views of mountain passes like Col de Coupe, river valleys such as the Neste and Aure, and charming traditional villages with slate roofs.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37661292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Couret d'Asque – View of Laborde loop from Avezac&lt;/a&gt;, a 20.7-mile (33.4 km) trail through the Pyrenean foothills. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37626885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gargas Cave – Saint-Bertrand-de-Comminges loop from La Barthe-de-Neste&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 41.5-kilometer ride.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the routes?
A: While cycling, you can explore several historical and natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2781837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Coupe&lt;/a&gt; offers beautiful views, and the Cistercian &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2563256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abbaye de l'Escaladieu&lt;/a&gt; provides a serene stop. You might also encounter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3540162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mauvezin Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a medieval fortress, or ride along the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2879024" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road of the Baronnies&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in La Barthe-De-Neste?
A: The region is excellent for cycling from spring through autumn. Summer offers warm weather ideal for exploring, especially the higher passes. However, the rolling countryside and valleys are pleasant in spring and early autumn, with vibrant colors and generally milder temperatures.

Q: How long do the touring cycling routes typically take to complete?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37626885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gargas Cave – Saint-Bertrand-de-Comminges loop from La Barthe-de-Neste&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 2 hours 30 minutes, while the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26227212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palomières Pass – Col de Coupe loop from Lannemezan&lt;/a&gt; can take over 5 hours 50 minutes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in La Barthe-De-Neste?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse terrain, from peaceful valley roads to challenging Pyrenean climbs, and the stunning views of the mountains and countryside.

Q: Are there options for less crowded touring cycling routes?
A: While popular routes can see more traffic, the region's extensive network of local cycling paths and smaller roads, particularly those mentioned by local cycling clubs like 'Le Grand Tour des Baronnies' or 'La Vallée de Neste,' often provide quieter alternatives away from the main thoroughfares. Exploring these can lead to a more tranquil experience.
tips: A stretch of road so pleasant to ride. To be included in a bucolic itinerary.
